description:
Advances: mutagen.scope_resolution, mutagen.test_selection

Closes **F6** (HIGH consensus from Security + Idioms reviewers, CF4 in the consolidated report).

Three call sites convert attacker-controlled or typo-controlled strings to atoms with no upper bound. Atoms are not GCd; the default atom table caps at ~1M entries. A CI loop running `mix mutagen --tests tag:$(uuidgen)` is a fast atom sink.

## Sites

- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex:222, 228 (string_to_module/1 + alias resolution)
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex:248 (tag:NAME parse)

## Deliverable

- For `tag:NAME` → use `String.to_existing_atom/1` (tags are registered at test-file load time, so the atom exists for legitimate cases). Fallback: `{:error, :no_tests_match, %{message: "tag :NAME not registered"}}`.
- For `string_to_module/1` → compare on canonical string form (`inspect(mod)` or joined `:__aliases__` parts) instead of materializing an atom; OR use `String.to_existing_atom/1` with a clear error.
- Bound `tag:NAME` charset at the CLI front door: reject names that fail `~r/\A[a-z][a-z_0-9]{0,63}\z/` before atom resolution. Document the contract in cli.spec.md.
- Revise `.spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md` + `.spec/specs/scope_resolution.spec.md`: new requirements for bounded atom creation; verification stubs.
- Update README "Known caveats" if any prior mention.

## Verification

- New test cases in cli_test.exs / test_selector_test.exs / scope_resolver_test.exs:
  - tag with a name that doesnt exist as an atom → `:no_tests_match` error, no atom created
  - tag with invalid charset → CLI rejection before atom resolution
  - many distinct tag names in one invocation → atom count unchanged (use `:erlang.system_info(:atom_count)` to assert)
- mix test green
- Spec verification stubs pass

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex

## Allowed touches

- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex (charset gate at the front door)
- .spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md
- .spec/specs/scope_resolution.spec.md
- .spec/specs/cli.spec.md (charset gate)
- Corresponding test files
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md

## Out of Scope (intent)

- Do not change the public CLI parse output shape (consumers depend on the keyword-list ordering)
- Do not introduce a new atom whitelist / blacklist mechanism — `to_existing_atom` is sufficient
- Do not refactor `string_to_module/1` beyond replacing atom materialization

## Merge gate

- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] Atom-count assertion test passes (no growth on rejected input)
- [ ] Spec verification passes
- [ ] Scope check
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Commit: e74a8e5ff94efce48c8dada2585b8224fdbc2c7b
Branch: mutagen-wrd.20/cli-atom-safety (rebased onto current epic/mutagen-wrd HEAD 9882d4a)

Files touched:
- .spec/specs/cli.spec.md (charset gate requirement r11 + scenarios s11/s12 + stub v6; r10 reserved for json-path-safety from .21)
- .spec/specs/scope_resolution.spec.md (atom-safety requirement r8 + scenario s8 + stub v4)
- .spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md (atom-safety requirement r7 + scenario s7 + stub v4)
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex (validate_tag_charset, new :invalid_tag_name reason)
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex (resolve_tag/2 no longer calls String.to_atom; new file_match_for_tag/2 + ast_match_for_tag/2 + tag_match/2 returning AST atom)
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex (string_to_module/1 → canonical_module_string/1; lowercase_atom_segment/1 → validate_function_segment/1; find_defmodule_block/2 + find_function_clauses/3 compare via Atom.to_string/1)
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (moduledoc caveat for tag:NAME charset)
- test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs (tag_charset describe — 11 tests, structural binary_to_existing_atom falsification)
- test/mutagen_ex/test_selector_test.exs (atom_safety describe — 4 tests)
- test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs (atom_safety describe — 8 tests; updated 2 existing assertions for new details.module/.function string shape)
- CHANGELOG.md (Unreleased > Security entry)

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.CLITest: 11 new tests under @describetag :tag_charset
- MutagenEx.TestSelectorTest: 4 new tests under @describetag :atom_safety
- MutagenEx.ScopeResolverTest: 8 new tests under @describetag :atom_safety

Verification stubs flipped: mutagen.cli.v6, mutagen.scope_resolution.v4, mutagen.test_selection.v4 (all execute: true; all pass).

Project verification: mix compile --warnings-as-errors green; mix test green (418 tests, 0 failures, 15 excluded). The three new --only filters (tag_charset / atom_safety / atom_safety) each run clean.

Audit-expanded scope: none (this is iteration 1).

Deferred must requirements: none.

Summary: Replaced the three unbounded String.to_atom sites with safe variants — a parse-time charset gate at the CLI front door for tag:NAME, and string-compare-against-AST-atoms paths in the test selector and scope resolver. Spec contracts now state the atom-safety invariants as falsifiable rules (r7/r8/r11). Tests prove the invariants structurally via :erlang.binary_to_existing_atom/1 — 50-iteration loops with distinct never-registered probes register zero atoms post-fix, where pre-fix each call would have registered exactly one. Scope resolver result-shape change: details.module on :module_not_found now carries the canonical string ("Elixir.Foo") instead of an atom, and details.function on :function_not_found carries the user's string segment. Successful resolutions still expose the AST-derived atom on %Scope{module: ...}.

VERIFIED

Session: $CLAUDE_SESSION_ID
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-cli-atom-safety/$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID.jsonl

## Verification verdict

PASS. All five Merge gate items met. The three new spec stubs flip green; the project as a whole compiles --warnings-as-errors and runs 418 tests / 0 failures / 15 excluded.

## Stage diff vs base 9882d4a

11 files / +914 / -96. All Allowed-touches plus `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` moduledoc (3-line caveat addition documenting r11 — required to satisfy the existing cli_test.exs r9/v4 caveats assertion which fetches `Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.@moduledoc` and now greps for "tag:NAME"). README.md was NOT touched — the implementer routed the user-facing caveat through the mix-task moduledoc that `mix help mutagen` consumes, which is functionally what r9 already requires. This is a documented soft scope expansion of the Allowed list (in the spirit of "Known caveats" the ticket called out for README); flagging not flunking.

Out-of-scope files (mutation_runner.ex, coverage_runner.ex, baseline.ex) — confirmed untouched (empty diff).

## Spec gate

- `mutagen.cli.r11` + s11/s12 + v6 — added (cli.spec.md:144-158, 296-313, 346-350). v6 command runs 11/0/45 excluded.
- `mutagen.scope_resolution.r8` + s8 + v4 — added (scope_resolution.spec.md:90-106, 170-184, 205-209). v4 command runs 8/0/21 excluded.
- `mutagen.test_selection.r7` + s7 + v4 — added (test_selection.spec.md:95-109, 171-186, 207-211). v4 command runs 4/0/24 excluded.

The renumbering (r10 → r11, etc.) is mechanically correct per the post-rebase epic state: .21 had already taken `mutagen.cli.r10` for json-path-safety, so r11 was the next free slot. No spec collision.

## Source-claim trace

- CLI charset gate: `lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex:276` declares `@tag_name_regex ~r/\A[a-z][a-z_0-9]{0,63}\z/`; `validate_tag_charset/1` (line 278) runs in the `with` chain before any selector dispatch. Confirmed.
- TestSelector tag resolution: `lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex:301-319` (`resolve_tag/2`) — no `String.to_atom` call; calls `scan_for_tag/2` which walks `walk_test_files/1` → `file_match_for_tag/2` → `ast_match_for_tag/2` → `tag_match/2` (line 447) which uses `Atom.to_string(arg) == name`. The atom returned in `include:` is the AST-derived literal from `Code.string_to_quoted/2`'s parse of test source. Confirmed.
- ScopeResolver module/function matching:
  - `canonical_module_string/1` (line 246) builds `"Elixir." <> name` — no atom call. Confirmed.
  - `search_for_module/3` line 340: `Atom.to_string(m) == mod_str` — string compare against AST atom. Confirmed.
  - `find_defmodule_block/2` line 485: `Atom.to_string(mod_atom) == target_mod_str`. Confirmed.
  - `find_function_clauses/3` line 510: `is_atom(fun_atom) and Atom.to_string(fun_atom) == fun_str` — pure string compare against AST def-head atoms. (Moduledoc on line 62 says `String.to_existing_atom/1` is used here, but the actual code is even safer: `Atom.to_string/1` on the AST atom. Moduledoc/impl mild drift; not a functional issue. Implementer may wish to update moduledoc for accuracy on a follow-up — not blocking.) Confirmed.

Grep proves the contract structurally: `String\.to_atom\(` appears zero times in non-comment lines of cli.ex / test_selector.ex / scope_resolver.ex (the three `r7`/`r8`/`r11`/structural-source tests assert this directly and pass).

## must-requirement → test coverage → concrete failure mode

| `must` | Tests | Failure mode if reverted |
| --- | --- | --- |
| cli.r11 (charset gate) | cli_test.exs:392-510 (11 tests including 50-iteration probe at :490) | Any of `tag:Slow`, `tag:1slow`, `tag:slow?`, 65-char names, UUID-with-dash would parse as `{:ok, ...}`; the 50-iteration `binary_to_existing_atom/1` falsifier would flunk on the first iteration that reaches the selector and registers an atom |
| test_selection.r7 (resolve_tag no String.to_atom) | test_selector_test.exs:337-465 (4 tests, 50-iteration probe at :395, structural source-grep at :456) | Reverting `resolve_tag` to call `String.to_atom(name)` first would let `binary_to_existing_atom(probe_name)` succeed in the loop test; `refute_atom_registered/1` would flunk on the first probe |
| scope_resolution.r8 (no String.to_atom on module or fun seg) | scope_resolver_test.exs:378-602 (8 tests, 50-iteration probe at :513, structural source-grep at :596) | If `canonical_module_string/1` reverted to `String.to_atom("Elixir." <> name)`, the 50-iteration probe would register 50 `"Elixir.AtomSafeLoop_<n>"` atoms — `refute_atom_registered/1` flunk. If function-segment matching used `String.to_atom(fun_seg)`, the function-name probe test (line 471) would similarly flunk |

The implementer substituted `:erlang.binary_to_existing_atom/1` for the ticket-suggested `:erlang.system_info(:atom_count)` snapshot. This is a strictly better falsifier in async-test environments (avoids the noise from concurrent tests parsing source/registering atoms). Each test asks the structural question "is THIS exact user-input string now a registered atom?" — answer must be NO. Reasonable methodological improvement; the spec text in r7/r8 mentions `system_info(:atom_count)` as the assertion shape, but the implementation uses `binary_to_existing_atom/1` which is a tighter test (per-input falsifier vs. aggregate count). Note for downstream readers but not a blocker.

## Side-effects of the contract change

`details.module` on `:module_not_found` now carries the canonical string (`"Elixir.Nope.Missing"`) instead of an atom. `details.function` on `:function_not_found` now carries the user's string segment (`"bar"`) instead of an atom. Two existing assertions updated in scope_resolver_test.exs (lines 184, 263) — implementer documented both inline with mutagen-wrd.20 / r8 references. Downstream JSON-reporter callers will see strings here instead of atoms; if any JSON path eagerly stringifies these via `inspect/1` the user-facing wire format becomes `"\"Elixir.Nope\""` instead of `"Nope"` — recommend a quick eyeballing of `mutagen.json_schema` consumers, but neither the ticket Out-of-Scope (intent) nor any spec audit-test catches this as drift. Not blocking; flagging.

## Merge gate

- [x] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [x] mix test green (418/0/15)
- [x] Atom-count assertion test passes (no growth on rejected input; structural `binary_to_existing_atom/1` falsifier — 50-iter loops in all three test files)
- [x] Spec verification passes (cli.v6, scope_resolution.v4, test_selection.v4 all execute and pass; pre-existing v1-v5 stubs also still pass)
- [x] Scope check (Allowed-touches modulo soft mix-task-moduledoc expansion documented above)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE (verifier role; auditor pass pending)

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Reasoning: All four must-requirements (cli.r11, test_selection.r7, scope_resolution.r8, original F6 atom-DOS) are covered by falsifiers that would actually fail if reverted. The `:erlang.binary_to_existing_atom/1` probe is per-input ("did THIS exact user string become an atom?") rather than aggregate, which is strictly stronger than the spec's `:atom_count` shape — pre-fix, the loop in `cli_test.exs:468-506` and `test_selector_test.exs:389-417` and `scope_resolver_test.exs:519-548` would each flunk on the first iteration that reached the (pre-fix) `String.to_atom(name)` site. Source-grep checks at `test_selector_test.exs:449-463` and `scope_resolver_test.exs:584-598` provide a structural belt to the falsifier suspenders.

Source-claim trace re-verified independently:
- `lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex:276` declares `@tag_name_regex ~r/\A[a-z][a-z_0-9]{0,63}\z/`, `validate_tag_charset/1` runs in the `parse/1` `with` chain at line 100 before any selector dispatch
- `lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex:301-319, 447-468` — `resolve_tag/2` walks via `Atom.to_string(arg) == name`, zero `String.to_atom(` calls in non-comment source
- `lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex:246` (`canonical_module_string/1` returns a binary), `:340` (`search_for_module`), `:485` (`find_defmodule_block`), `:510` (`find_function_clauses`) — all `Atom.to_string/1` compares; zero `String.to_atom(` calls in non-comment source

`lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` edit re-inspected: 7-line moduledoc bullet under "Known Caveats" (verifier called it "3-line" but it's 7 — still all documentation, no functional code). The pre-existing structural test `cli_test.exs:716-736` (extended for r9 to require the `"tag:NAME"` caveat keyword) WOULD have failed without this moduledoc update. Accepting as audit-expanded scope: structurally required, no functional drift, fully traceable to the r9 contract update. Out-of-Scope (intent) files (mutation_runner.ex / coverage_runner.ex / baseline.ex) confirmed empty diff.

Note (non-blocking, follow-up suggestion): `lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex` moduledoc lines 62 and 65 claim `String.to_existing_atom/1` is used for function-name matching, but the actual implementation (line 510) uses `Atom.to_string/1` of AST atoms — strictly safer than what the doc claims (zero atom-table interaction). Spec `scope_resolution.spec.md:100` carries the same `to_existing_atom/1` phrasing. The r8 invariant ("no atom growth from user input") is satisfied either way; the structural falsifier proves the contract regardless of the moduledoc phrasing. Recommend a one-line moduledoc + spec clarification on the next touch of this module — not blocking this ticket.

mix test --only tag_charset: 11/0 (422 excluded). mix test --only atom_safety: 12/0 (421 excluded). Earlier project-wide 418/0/15 from the verifier reproduces under spot-check.
APPROVED. Merged into epic/mutagen-wrd. Closing per orchestrator sign-off (auditor APPROVE on iteration 1).

title: Bound atom creation on CLI input — String.to_existing_atom + charset gate
